channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-01
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. In the `scienceverse/metacheck` project, the `ref_update` branch has been the focus of attention, with updates to `ref_accuracy`, `reg_check`, and `ref_consistency`, as well as improvements to error logging. Meanwhile, `Davidvandijcke/coarse` has made strides in localization and deployment fixes, while `JamieCummins/regcheck` has removed the sign-up feature from its `staging` branch. Notably, `giladfeldman/escicheck` has synchronized changes from the `escicheckapp` repository, bringing in updates from `effectcheck` version `0.6.11`. These connections between projects demonstrate the collaborative spirit of our community, and we celebrate the progress being made towards our shared goals. As our projects continue to evolve, we look forward to seeing the impact of these updates and the new developments on the horizon.

39 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Most active:

regcheck20 commits
metacheck7 commits
docpluck7 commits
escicheck5 commits

item.title: metacheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `ref_update` branch was the focus of recent work, where a commit on July 1, 2026, modified the code to "prevent double crossref api, log errors", as stated in the commit message. On June 30, 2026, in the same `ref_update` branch, the `ref_accuracy` was updated and validated, according to the commit message. Additionally, on June 30, 2026, "Minor fixes to reg_check and ref_consistency" were made, as specified in the commit message. No other branches were worked on during this period, with all changes occurring on the `ref_update` branch. The `ref_update` branch saw changes to error logging, `ref_accuracy`, `reg_check`, and `ref_consistency`.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 41a54be [ref_update] prevent double crossref api, log errors — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 7 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The `staging` branch was worked on, where the commit removed the sign-up feature, as indicated by the message "remove sign-up". The change specifically targeted the removal of this functionality, with no other branches mentioned as being affected by this commit. No other branches, such as `main` or `dev`, were mentioned in the provided commits, so it is unclear if this change is unique to `staging`. This removal was done by Jamie Cummins, as attributed in the commit message. There were no other commits mentioned, so the full scope of this change is limited to the `staging` branch and the removal of sign-up. The removal of sign-up from the `staging` branch is the only modification noted in the given commits.

7 commit(s) today:

  • 1f6986b [staging] remove sign-up — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that synchronized changes from the `escicheckapp` repository at commit `928a5bc`, which corresponds to `effectcheck` version `0.6.11`, as indicated by the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11)". This commit is associated with issue `#24`. No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The specific changes brought in by this synchronization are not detailed in the commit message, but they are tied to the `effectcheck` version `0.6.11`. The commit message does not mention any new features, fixes, or modules added directly in the `giladfeldman/escicheck` repository.

1 commit(s) today:

  • bedc262 [main] sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11) (#24) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
